sourceMovie.title: Brazil
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Brazil (1985) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Terry Gilliam's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Idiocracy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Brazil clicked for you, Idiocracy (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and science fiction, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Brazil?
faq.answer: Try Idiocracy, Sleeper, They Cloned Tyrone—each shares DNA with Brazil while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Brazil?
faq.answer: Brazil (1985) is best described as Comedy, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Brazil worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
